title: Atlanta Crime Fell 70% From Its 1989 Peak — Then the Data Changed. 242 Neighborhoods, Mapped
titleOptions: 88,536 to 26,995: Atlanta's Long Crime Fall — and the Two Years With No Data
40 Years of Atlanta Crime in 5 Minutes — Every Number Sourced, Every Gap Disclosed
description:
Forty years of reported crime in Atlanta — a 70% fall from the 1989 peak (88,536 index crimes) to 2018 (26,995) under the FBI's measure, two transition years with no comparable data (2019–2020, shown as a gap, never interpolated), then APD's own NIBRS record across all 242 official neighborhoods.

This is a fully sourced, reproducible data visualization. Every figure comes straight from the FBI Uniform Crime Reporting program and the Atlanta Police Department's open NIBRS data. Every dot on the map is a real recorded offense location at block-level addresses. Coverage and caveats are shown on screen — including the November 2024 one-month tripling of 'crimes against society', flagged on screen as a recording-pattern change rather than a street-level wave.

The FBI's classic 'index' crimes (through 2018) and APD's NIBRS records (2021+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, never merged into one line. The step at the seam is the ruler changing, not a crime wave, and the 2019–2020 hole is disclosed, not papered over.

▶ CHAPTERS
0:00  Hook — the interrupted fall
0:08  What you're about to see
0:22  Chapter 1 — the long arc
2:30  The last five years — up close
2:43  Chapter 2 — the map, month by month
4:52  The busiest & safest areas
5:18  Sources & credits

▶ MADE BY AN AI — FULLY TRANSPARENT
This video was produced end-to-end by Claude (Anthropic's AI): it located and fetched the official source data, built and validated the data pipeline, verified every on-screen figure against the sources, designed and rendered the visuals, and generated the music. No human edited the numbers. Every step — code, data, and design decisions — is public and reproducible in the repository linked below. If you find an error, open an issue; corrections are part of the record.

▶ CODE, DATA & FULL SOURCES (reproduce every number)
https://github.com/hadi-nayebi/crime-cartography/tree/main/videos/atlanta-ga

Music generated with Stable Audio Open (Stability AI). Basemap © OpenStreetMap contributors.
